ground_truth.title: The New Rent-A-Car: Changes in the Rental Car Industry
ground_truth.author: Ivan Lequerica
ground_truth.main_content:
A new mobility paradigm is just beginning. City dwellers have innumerable choices of transportation — car sharing, ride hailing, scooters, bikes and others. Some original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) state that they are not car manufacturers anymore but mobility solution providers. This changing scenario represents a unique opportunity for businesses in the rental car industry to step up and become dominant players. Rent-a-car companies have the channel, the brand and the relationship with end customers needed to succeed in today's world of connected vehicles.

Leading rent-a-car companies have a connected car plan in place because of the many benefits that telematics solutions present. Most often they have two objectives when approaching connected car telematics solutions: optimize challenges in their current operations and commercialization channels, and get prepared for new monetization scenarios and business models based on the data they can gather through telematics.

Rent-a-car companies usually have two lines of business: B2B (business to business) and B2C (business to consumer). In B2B, they rent fleets for other businesses. In B2C, they rent, typically passenger cars, to general consumers. The B2B model normally implies longer rental periods and the benefits of using telematics are similar to the ones in the leasing and fleet management industry.

A rent-a-car company has a multitude of processes that can be grouped in two categories, depending on the vehicle state: on rental and off rental. On rental is the period of a vehicle when the car is rented out by a customer. It starts when a driver rents the vehicle and ends when she/he returns it and gets billed. In the off rental state, the vehicle is not generating revenue and the objective is to optimize all the processes from the driver returning the vehicle until is ready to be rented again.

Key telematics use cases include: fuel consumption tracking with accurate data from OEM tank level sensors; automating the close of contract process for faster vehicle inspection; vehicle location services for emergency response and collision detection; monitoring employee vehicle use during off-rental periods; managing stock and vehicle location by VIN; maintenance management through real-time diagnostic trouble codes; and remarketing vehicles based on optimal residual value timing. Studies performed by Geotab with rent-a-car customers indicate that the ROI, taking into account all the scenarios, can represent more than 20€ (or $23 USD) per vehicle each month on savings.
with: A new mobility paradigm is just beginning. City dwellers have innumerable choices of transportation — car sharing, ride hailing, scooters, bikes and others.
Leading rent-a-car companies have a connected car plan in place because of the many benefits that telematics solutions present.
With diagnostic fleet tracking solution like Geotab, which collects engine data, rent-a-car companies can collect accurate data from the OEM tank level sensor.

In the unfortunate event of a collision, drivers can be confused on how they should proceed. The ability to detect vehicle collisions but also breakdowns allows the rent-a-car operations team to manage the situation efficiently.
Studies performed by Geotab with rent-a-car customers indicate that the ROI, taking into account all the scenarios, can represent more than 20€ (or $23 USD) per vehicle each month on savings.
